metadata.title: Context Engineering for AI-Augmented Development
metadata.description: Curated sources for context-driven development with AI coding agents across single and multi-repo organizations. Covers context engineering foundations, AGENTS.md standard, paradigm shift evidence, tool documentation, multi-repo patterns, regulatory compliance, security tooling, and practitioner insights.
metadata.verification_policy: Prefer official vendor docs, regulator pages, and primary research for volatile claims. Mark high-volatility sources explicitly.

knowledge_graph_methodology.name: Understand-Anything: Multi-Agent Knowledge Graph Pipeline
knowledge_graph_methodology.description: Multi-agent pipeline using tree-sitter AST and LLM semantic enrichment to build JSON knowledge graphs from codebases. Adoptable patterns: graph-as-artifact (committable JSON), BFS traversal for focused context windows, 7-check automated validation, two-pass analysis (deterministic + LLM).
knowledge_graph_methodology.relevance: Influenced the context-graph schema and scan_context_artifacts.py design — specifically the graph-as-artifact pattern and validation check framework

context_engineering.name: Anthropic: Effective Context Engineering for AI Agents
context_engineering.description: Anthropic's official guide to context engineering for coding agents. Covers context retrieval, memory, tools, planning, and execution patterns.
context_engineering.relevance: Foundational framework for context design principles used throughout this skill

context_engineering.name: Martin Fowler: Context Engineering for Coding Agents
context_engineering.description: Fowler's analysis of context engineering as an emerging discipline. Covers structured prompting, codebase context, and progressive disclosure.
context_engineering.relevance: Industry validation from a respected voice — establishes CE as engineering, not hacking

context_engineering.name: The Context Development Lifecycle (CDLC)
context_engineering.description: Generate-Evaluate-Distribute-Observe lifecycle for context artifacts. The continuous improvement loop for keeping context fresh.
context_engineering.relevance: Core CDLC framework referenced throughout this skill — the feedback loop that makes CE sustainable

context_engineering.name: Andrej Karpathy: Observations on Agentic Coding
context_engineering.description: Karpathy's observations on disciplined agentic coding patterns — surfacing assumptions, managing scope, pushing back on bad approaches
context_engineering.relevance: Source material for the canonical behavioral rules maintained in agents-memory: frameworks/shared-skills/skills/agents-memory/references/coding-behavior.md (standalone file) and frameworks/shared-skills/skills/agents-memory/references/memory-examples.md §Example 6 (inline template). The repo-root .claude/rules/coding-behavior.md is a symlink to the canonical file.

derivative_source.name: forrestchang/andrej-karpathy-skills
refinements_applied: trace test (every changed line traces to user request)
orphan vs pre-existing dead code distinction

context_engineering.name: karpathy/autoresearch
context_engineering.description: Autonomous ML experiment agent with bounded modification surface, fixed eval metric, and git-as-experiment-ledger. The original autonomous improvement loop pattern.
context_engineering.relevance: Reference implementation for autonomous context improvement — defines the change→eval→keep/revert loop applied to CDLC

context_engineering.name: Simon Willison: Context Engineering Practices
context_engineering.description: Willison's ongoing documentation of context engineering patterns, tool comparisons, and practical tips
context_engineering.relevance: Practitioner-level insights on what works and what doesn't in daily CE practice

context_engineering.name: Sourcegraph: The Death of the Junior Developer (and the Rise of the Context Engineer)
context_engineering.description: Analysis of how AI agents change the role of developers, with context engineering as the new core skill
context_engineering.relevance: Role transformation evidence — supports paradigm-comparison.md role evolution section

context_engineering.name: Anthropic: Claude Code Memory and CLAUDE.md
context_engineering.description: Official Claude Code documentation for memory files, imports, project memory, and directory-scoped guidance.
context_engineering.relevance: Authoritative guide for CLAUDE.md structure, imports, and repo-level memory patterns

context_engineering.name: Codified Context: Infrastructure for AI Agents in a Complex Codebase
context_engineering.description: Three-tier memory architecture (hot constitution → specialist agents → cold MCP knowledge base) for 108K LOC codebase. 283 sessions over 70 days. Context infrastructure = 24.2% of codebase. Key finding: single-file manifests cannot scale beyond modest codebases.
context_engineering.relevance: Research validation of tiered context architecture — supports maturity progression from L1 (single file) to L3+ (agents + knowledge base)

context_engineering.name: Manus: Context Engineering for AI Agents — Lessons from Building Manus
context_engineering.description: Production patterns: KV-cache hit rate as primary metric (10x cost difference), masking over removal, file system as extended memory, attention through recitation (todo.md), preserving failure states, avoiding few-shot brittleness.
context_engineering.relevance: Advanced context optimization patterns applicable to CDLC Observe phase — cost and performance tuning

context_engineering.name: Context Development Lifecycle: Your AI Agent Isn't Broken, Your Context Is
context_engineering.description: External validation of CDLC framework (Generate → Evaluate → Distribute → Observe). Key insight: 'Every new session = onboarding a hire who knows programming but nothing about your project.' Adds test-driven context development concept.
context_engineering.relevance: Independent validation of CDLC framework — confirms Generate-Evaluate-Distribute-Observe as industry-recognized lifecycle

agents_md_standard.name: AGENTS.md Specification
agents_md_standard.description: The AGENTS.md standard for repository agent instructions. Defines file format, sections, and cross-tool compatibility.
agents_md_standard.relevance: Primary standard this entire skill builds upon — AGENTS.md is the source of truth

agents_md_standard.name: AGENTS.md GitHub Repository
agents_md_standard.description: Official repository for the AGENTS.md specification with examples and tooling
agents_md_standard.relevance: Reference implementation and examples of well-structured AGENTS.md files

agents_md_standard.name: GitHub Docs: Repository Custom Instructions and AGENTS.md
agents_md_standard.description: Official GitHub documentation for repository instructions, path-specific instructions, and agent instruction files including AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, and GEMINI.md.
agents_md_standard.relevance: Primary-source evidence that GitHub/Copilot now supports layered repository context instead of a single file convention

agents_md_standard.name: Agentic AI Foundation Stewardship via AGENTS.md
agents_md_standard.description: The AGENTS.md site documents stewardship under the Agentic AI Foundation and provides the current specification and FAQ.
agents_md_standard.relevance: Use this instead of outdated secondary references when discussing AGENTS.md governance or stewardship

agents_md_standard.name: AGENTS.md Adoption Tracker
agents_md_standard.description: GitHub search showing AGENTS.md adoption across public repositories
agents_md_standard.relevance: Use only as a rough directional signal; do not rely on this search page for stable adoption metrics

agents_md_standard.name: ETH Zurich: Evaluating AGENTS.md — Are Context Files Helpful for Coding Agents?
agents_md_standard.description: Empirical evaluation of context file effectiveness. 138 tasks, 4 models. LLM-generated files degrade performance by 3% and increase costs 20%+. Human-written files provide marginal +4% but raise costs 19%. Recommends limiting to non-inferable details only.
agents_md_standard.relevance: Critical research caveat for AGENTS.md authoring — validates 'quality over quantity' principle and progressive disclosure pattern

paradigm_shift.name: OpenAI: Harness Engineering
paradigm_shift.description: OpenAI's framework for organizing engineering work around AI agents — structured task descriptions, verification, feedback loops
paradigm_shift.relevance: Codex-side perspective on context-driven development; validates dual-agent approach

paradigm_shift.name: GitHub spec-kit
paradigm_shift.description: GitHub's toolkit for spec-driven AI development — templates, validation, best practices. Specs live in .specify/specs/<feature>/ as spec.md, plan.md, tasks.md. Install via uv tool install specify-cli.
paradigm_shift.relevance: L3 artifact pipeline in framework-selection-matrix; most-cited spec-driven framework as of April 2026 (~92k stars, GitHub-backed)

paradigm_shift.name: OpenSpec (Fission-AI)
paradigm_shift.description: Community spec-driven framework. Specs live in openspec/changes/ as proposal.md, spec.md, design.md, tasks.md. Installs via npm i -g @fission-ai/openspec; commands /opsx:propose, /opsx:apply, /opsx:archive. Integrates ADRs alongside specs.
paradigm_shift.relevance: L3 artifact pipeline alternative to Spec Kit in framework-selection-matrix; npm-native, ADRs co-located

paradigm_shift.name: obra/superpowers
paradigm_shift.description: Skills-based engineering methodology shipped as a folder of Markdown files. 14 documented skills (brainstorming, writing-plans, TDD, systematic-debugging, subagent-driven-development, two-stage code review, …). Plugin configs for Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode, Copilot CLI, Gemini CLI. Skills enforce discipline at runtime (refuse-to-code-until-clarified).
paradigm_shift.relevance: L2 methodology layer in framework-selection-matrix; pick when discipline drift (TDD, debugging quality) is the bottleneck. ~150-175k stars, on Anthropic plugin marketplace.

paradigm_shift.name: gsd-build/get-shit-done (GSD)
paradigm_shift.description: Context-engineering and lifecycle framework targeting context rot — quality degradation as the context window fills. Full mode: 59 skills + 33 subagents; minimal mode: 6 skills. Generates PROJECT.md, REQUIREMENTS.md, ROADMAP.md, STATE.md, phase docs. Installs via npx get-shit-done-cc@latest. Ships git hooks for automated context management.
paradigm_shift.relevance: L2 lifecycle / context-management layer in framework-selection-matrix; pick when context rot in long sessions is the bottleneck. ~59k stars.

paradigm_shift.name: Anthropic Agent Skills overview
paradigm_shift.description: Anthropic's official skill system. A skill is a directory with SKILL.md (YAML frontmatter + instructions) plus optional scripts and reference files. Three-level progressive disclosure: metadata always loaded (~100 tokens), instructions on trigger (<5k tokens), bundled resources on demand. Personal skills at ~/.claude/skills/, project skills at .claude/skills/.
paradigm_shift.relevance: L2 capability layer foundation in framework-selection-matrix; the substrate superpowers, GSD, and custom skills build on.

paradigm_shift.name: AGENTS.md spec
paradigm_shift.description: Canonical spec for the AGENTS.md portable baseline. Single Markdown file at repo root that all major agents (Codex, Claude Code, Amp, Jules, Cursor, Factory) read natively. 32 KiB Codex limit. Stewarded by the Linux Foundation's Agentic AI Foundation.
paradigm_shift.relevance: L0 portable baseline in framework-selection-matrix; mandatory layer for cross-tool consistency

paradigm_shift.name: Cursor rules documentation
paradigm_shift.description: Per-project and per-path instruction files for Cursor IDE using .mdc (Markdown Cursor) format with YAML frontmatter for conditional activation. Legacy .cursorrules root file deprecated. Global rules at ~/.cursor/rules/.
paradigm_shift.relevance: L1 runtime-specific layer in framework-selection-matrix; mandatory for Cursor-primary teams (Cursor does not read AGENTS.md natively)

paradigm_shift.name: GitHub Copilot custom instructions
paradigm_shift.description: Copilot repo-wide instructions at .github/copilot-instructions.md. Path-specific instructions via .github/**/*.instructions.md with applyTo frontmatter (added July 2025). Agent-specific instructions added November 2025.
paradigm_shift.relevance: L1 runtime-specific layer in framework-selection-matrix; mandatory for Copilot-primary teams

paradigm_shift.name: Cline rules documentation
paradigm_shift.description: .clinerules file or .clinerules/ directory injected into Cline's system prompt on every request. Inactive rules at clinerules-bank/. v3.13 added a per-rule toggle UI.
paradigm_shift.relevance: L1 runtime-specific layer in framework-selection-matrix; required for Cline (VS Code extension) workflows

paradigm_shift.name: Aider conventions
paradigm_shift.description: CONVENTIONS.md loaded into every Aider session via .aider.conf.yml. Documents language preferences, style, testing standards, git practices. Community-editable conventions at github.com/Aider-AI/conventions.
paradigm_shift.relevance: L1 runtime-specific layer in framework-selection-matrix; required for Aider pair-programming workflows

paradigm_shift.name: Martin Fowler / Böckeler: SDD Tools — Three Levels of Spec-Driven Development
paradigm_shift.description: Defines three maturity levels for spec-driven development: Spec-First (write then discard), Spec-Anchored (spec lives alongside code), Spec-as-Source (spec is primary artifact, code regenerated). Evaluates tooling landscape.
paradigm_shift.relevance: Canonical SDD maturity framework referenced in the Spec-Driven Development section of this skill

paradigm_shift.name: Julián De Angelis: The Spec Is the New Code — A Guide to Spec Driven Development
paradigm_shift.description: SDD methodology guide from MercadoLibre's GenAI Technical Leader. Covers the ambiguity problem, ecosystem convergence (Spec Kit, Symphony, Ralph Loop), the specify-plan-task-implement process, tradeoffs (2-3x token cost for dramatically better results), and rollout to 20,000 developers.
paradigm_shift.relevance: Practitioner case study at scale — validates SDD methodology with 5,000+ workshop participants and production evidence

paradigm_shift.name: METR: Measuring AI Ability to Complete Long Tasks
paradigm_shift.description: Research showing unstructured AI coding ('vibe coding') is 19% slower with 1.7x more issues than structured approaches
paradigm_shift.relevance: Key evidence for why context engineering matters — data-backed case against vibe coding

paradigm_shift.name: Gartner: AI-Augmented Software Engineering Predictions
paradigm_shift.description: Gartner predictions on AI-augmented development — 75% of enterprise developers using AI assistants by 2028
paradigm_shift.relevance: Strategic context for organizational adoption planning

paradigm_shift.name: McKinsey: The Economic Potential of Generative AI in Software Engineering
paradigm_shift.description: McKinsey analysis of AI impact on developer productivity — 20-45% time savings on coding tasks
paradigm_shift.relevance: Business case data for CDLC investment and SDLC compression claims

paradigm_shift.name: Stack Overflow Developer Survey: AI Tools Adoption
paradigm_shift.description: Annual developer survey tracking AI tool adoption rates, tool preferences, and satisfaction
paradigm_shift.relevance: Industry benchmarks for AI tool adoption — useful for making the case internally

paradigm_shift.name: Google DeepMind: AI-Assisted Software Engineering Study
paradigm_shift.description: Google's research on AI coding agent productivity including Gemini-based development
paradigm_shift.relevance: Multi-vendor evidence for context-driven development effectiveness

paradigm_shift.name: Anthropic: 2026 Agentic Coding Trends Report
paradigm_shift.description: Eight trends reshaping software engineering: role shift to agent coordination, multi-agent teams, end-to-end agent runs (hours/days), agents learning to ask for help, spread beyond engineers, shorter timelines, security implications. AI in 60% of work but only 0-20% fully delegated.
paradigm_shift.relevance: Authoritative industry report validating paradigm shift — data-backed trend evidence from the primary agent platform

paradigm_shift.name: Shopify CEO: AI Hiring Freeze Memo
paradigm_shift.description: Shopify CEO Tobi Lutke's April 2025 internal memo: teams must demonstrate why AI cannot do the work before requesting headcount. AI usage added to performance reviews.
paradigm_shift.relevance: Highest-profile public statement of AI-first hiring policy — direct evidence for team size paradigm shift

paradigm_shift.name: Anthropic: How AI Is Transforming Work at Anthropic
paradigm_shift.description: Anthropic internal data: 90% of Claude Code written by Claude, engineers use Claude in 59% of work (up from 28%), 50% productivity boost, 5 PRs/engineer/day. Most employees can fully delegate only 0-20% of work.
paradigm_shift.relevance: Primary-source quantitative evidence from the leading agent platform — both the promise and the limits

paradigm_shift.name: Pragmatic Engineer: Future of Software Engineering with AI
paradigm_shift.description: Gergely Orosz on the 'one-pizza team' replacing the 'two-pizza team.' Predicts decrease in specialization, consolidation into 3-4 full-stack engineers. Warns AI makes teams 'dysfunctional faster' if culture is poor.
paradigm_shift.relevance: Origin of 'one-pizza team' taxonomy — key thought leader framing the shift with both optimism and caveats

paradigm_shift.name: Satya Nadella: AI Writes 30% of Microsoft's Code
paradigm_shift.description: Nadella at LlamaCon (April 2025): 30% of Microsoft code is AI-generated. Describes a 'new production function' where AI generates code without proportionally more engineers.
paradigm_shift.relevance: Direct evidence from a $3T company — validates the scale of AI code generation in big tech

paradigm_shift.name: CNBC: Y Combinator Startups Fastest Growing in Fund History
paradigm_shift.description: Y Combinator 2025 batch on pace to be fastest-growing in fund history. 88% of startups are AI-native. 25% have 95%+ AI-generated codebases.
paradigm_shift.relevance: VC ecosystem evidence for AI-native startup economics — validates 'lean team + AI' investment thesis

paradigm_shift.name: Every.to: The Two-Slice Team
paradigm_shift.description: Concept of the 'two-slice team' (1-2 people). Every.to runs 4 products each operated by 1 person with 99% AI-written code. Explores the economics and psychology of extreme team minimalism.
paradigm_shift.relevance: Origin of 'two-slice team' taxonomy — demonstrates the solo-operator model with real revenue data

paradigm_shift.name: Fortune: Klarna CEO Admits AI Cuts Went Too Far
paradigm_shift.description: Klarna CEO Sebastian Siemiatkowski: chatbot gave generic responses, couldn't handle nuance. Company began rehiring. Average pay rose 60% ($126K → $203K) as workforce skewed senior.
paradigm_shift.relevance: Critical counterpoint — the highest-profile AI-driven layoff reversal. Essential caveat for team transformation planning.

paradigm_shift.name: MLQ: Klarna CEO Regrets AI-Driven Layoffs
paradigm_shift.description: Analysis of Klarna's 2026 reversal: cut from 5,527 to 2,907 employees, AI replaced 853 FTEs, then admitted going too far. 55% of companies report regretting AI-driven layoffs.
paradigm_shift.relevance: Quantitative details on the Klarna reversal — the '55% regret rate' data point

paradigm_shift.name: Faros AI: The Real Impact of AI on Coding Productivity
paradigm_shift.description: Study of 10,000+ developers: high-AI teams completed 21% more tasks but PR review time increased 91%, bugs per developer up 9%, PR sizes up 154%. Bottleneck shifted from writing to reviewing.
paradigm_shift.relevance: Critical quantitative counterpoint — shows AI throughput gains come with review and quality costs

paradigm_shift.name: Lean AI Leaderboard: Revenue Per Employee
paradigm_shift.description: Tracks revenue-per-employee metrics for AI-native companies. AI-native average: $3.48M RPE vs traditional SaaS $200K-$610K (5.7x gap). Midjourney $4.1M, Cursor $3.3M.
paradigm_shift.relevance: Quantitative foundation for AI-native RPE claims and the team-size economic argument

paradigm_shift.name: Optimum Partners: Engineering Management in the Age of AI (2026)
paradigm_shift.description: Introduces the 'Centaur Pod' model: 3-person team (architect + 2 AI reliability engineers + agents). Predicts engineering management evolves from people management to agent orchestration.
paradigm_shift.relevance: Origin of 'Centaur Pod' taxonomy — specific team structure for AI-augmented engineering

paradigm_shift.name: Gartner: 80% of Engineering Workforce Must Upskill by 2027
paradigm_shift.description: Gartner prediction (Oct 2024): 80% of the software engineering workforce will need to upskill in AI-augmented development practices by 2027.
paradigm_shift.relevance: Sets the scale of the transition challenge — upskilling is non-trivial and affects team planning timelines

paradigm_shift.name: CNBC: Duolingo 'AI-First' Company
paradigm_shift.description: Duolingo's AI-first policy: contractors cut in waves, CEO reports '4 or 5 times as much content in the same time,' 148 AI-written courses delivered.
paradigm_shift.relevance: Evidence of AI-first transformation in a consumer tech company — content generation as a proxy for engineering output

paradigm_shift.name: Sam Altman: The One-Person Unicorn
paradigm_shift.description: Sam Altman's prediction that AI will enable solo founders to build billion-dollar companies. Origin of the 'one-person unicorn' concept.
paradigm_shift.relevance: Origin of the 'one-person unicorn' term — sets the aspirational endpoint of the team-size spectrum

paradigm_shift.name: Meta: Zuckerberg Predicts Mid-Level Engineer AI
paradigm_shift.description: Zuckerberg (Jan 2025): 'AI that can effectively be a mid-level engineer.' Meta cut 5% of staff while investing in AI infrastructure.
paradigm_shift.relevance: Big tech CEO framing AI as replacing mid-level engineering work — shapes hiring and team composition decisions

tool_documentation.name: Claude Code Documentation
tool_documentation.description: Official Claude Code CLI documentation — hooks, subagents, worktrees, MCP, memory files
tool_documentation.relevance: Primary agent tool — all CDLC patterns built on Claude Code capabilities

tool_documentation.name: Claude Code GitHub Repository
tool_documentation.description: Claude Code source repository with issues, discussions, and changelog
tool_documentation.relevance: Track feature updates, bug reports, and community patterns

tool_documentation.name: OpenAI: Introducing Codex
tool_documentation.description: OpenAI's Codex launch and product overview covering agentic coding workflows, repository setup, and execution model.
tool_documentation.relevance: Primary-source overview for Codex positioning and workflow assumptions

tool_documentation.name: Cursor Documentation
tool_documentation.description: Cursor IDE with AI integration — .cursor/rules, agent mode, context management
tool_documentation.relevance: Supplementary tool — reads .cursor/rules for IDE-level context

tool_documentation.name: GitHub Copilot Documentation
tool_documentation.description: GitHub Copilot documentation covering repository instructions, path-specific instructions, AGENTS.md support, coding agent, and custom agents.
tool_documentation.relevance: Primary-source documentation for GitHub-native context files and agent workflows

tool_documentation.name: Windsurf (Codeium) Documentation
tool_documentation.description: Windsurf IDE agent with Cascade — .windsurfrules context file support
tool_documentation.relevance: Alternative AI IDE — context patterns translate across tools

tool_documentation.name: Amazon Q Developer Documentation
tool_documentation.description: AWS AI coding assistant — workspace context, guardrails, enterprise features
tool_documentation.relevance: Enterprise alternative — relevant for AWS-heavy organizations evaluating tools

tool_documentation.name: Google Gemini CLI / Code Assist
tool_documentation.description: Google's CLI agent for coding — GEMINI.md context file, agent mode
tool_documentation.relevance: Third major agent platform — multi-agent strategy may include Gemini

tool_documentation.name: GitHub Docs: Enterprise Management for Agents
tool_documentation.description: Official GitHub documentation for enterprise agent management, governance controls, and organization-wide administration.
tool_documentation.relevance: Use this for GitHub enterprise governance claims instead of unstable branding or marketing names

tool_documentation.name: VS Code: Context Engineering Guide
tool_documentation.description: VS Code guidance for layered context: AGENTS.md, custom instructions, prompt files, and custom chat modes / agents.
tool_documentation.relevance: Primary-source evidence that editor workflows now converge on layered repository context

tool_documentation.name: context-hub (chub) — Curated API Docs and Skills for Coding Agents
tool_documentation.description: CLI tool providing agents with curated, versioned API documentation from a searchable CDN. Dual content model (Docs vs Skills), self-improving loop via annotations and feedback, progressive disclosure, multi-source trust signaling (official/maintainer/community), Agent Skills spec compatible. 500+ library entries.
tool_documentation.relevance: External knowledge distribution tool complementing repo-native context — fills the gap between AGENTS.md (repo context) and web search (unreliable). Supports the Cross-Tool Context Model's external knowledge layer.

multi_repo_patterns.name: Spine Pattern for Polyrepo Coordination
multi_repo_patterns.description: Coordination pattern for managing shared concerns across many repositories without coupling
multi_repo_patterns.relevance: Architectural pattern for 100+ repo organizations — basis for coordination repo pattern

multi_repo_patterns.name: InnerSource Patterns
multi_repo_patterns.description: Community patterns for internal open source practices — governance, contribution, review
multi_repo_patterns.relevance: Governance model for shared context artifacts across teams

multi_repo_patterns.name: GitHub Template Repositories
multi_repo_patterns.description: GitHub's template repository feature for standardizing new repo creation
multi_repo_patterns.relevance: Mechanism for Pattern 2 (Template Repository + Sync) in multi-repo strategy

multi_repo_patterns.name: Git Submodules Documentation
multi_repo_patterns.description: Official Git documentation for submodules — version-pinned shared code across repos
multi_repo_patterns.relevance: Distribution mechanism for shared context: version-pinned, explicit updates

multi_repo_patterns.name: Monorepo vs Polyrepo: Comparison and Strategies
multi_repo_patterns.description: Atlassian's comparison of monorepo vs polyrepo strategies with trade-offs
multi_repo_patterns.relevance: Background for choosing coordination pattern based on repo structure

multi_repo_patterns.name: GitHub Actions: Reusable Workflows
multi_repo_patterns.description: GitHub Actions reusable workflows for sharing CI/CD patterns across repositories
multi_repo_patterns.relevance: Mechanism for distributing compliance gates across 100 repos

security_tooling.name: Gitleaks: Secret Detection
security_tooling.description: SAST tool for detecting hardcoded secrets in git repos — API keys, tokens, passwords
security_tooling.relevance: Required compliance gate in fca-compliance-gate.yml — blocks merge on secrets

security_tooling.name: Semgrep
security_tooling.description: Lightweight static analysis for finding bugs, security issues, and enforcing code standards
security_tooling.relevance: SAST scanning in compliance gate — security-audit, secrets, OWASP top 10 rulesets

security_tooling.name: GitHub CodeQL
security_tooling.description: GitHub's semantic code analysis engine for finding security vulnerabilities
security_tooling.relevance: Alternative to Semgrep for SAST scanning in compliance gates

security_tooling.name: Snyk
security_tooling.description: Developer security platform — dependency scanning, container scanning, code analysis
security_tooling.relevance: Dependency vulnerability scanning in compliance gate; alternative to Dependabot

security_tooling.name: GitHub Dependabot
security_tooling.description: Automated dependency updates and security alerts for GitHub repositories
security_tooling.relevance: Dependency vulnerability scanning — complements gitleaks and SAST

security_tooling.name: OWASP Top 10
security_tooling.description: OWASP Top 10 web application security risks — the baseline security checklist
security_tooling.relevance: Referenced in Semgrep ruleset (p/owasp-top-ten); baseline for security rules

security_tooling.name: OWASP LLM Top 10
security_tooling.description: OWASP Top 10 risks specific to LLM applications — prompt injection, data leakage, etc.
security_tooling.relevance: AI-specific security risks relevant when using coding agents in regulated environments

security_tooling.name: NIST AI Risk Management Framework
security_tooling.description: NIST framework for managing AI system risks — governance, mapping, measuring, managing
security_tooling.relevance: Complements FCA requirements; US equivalent for AI risk governance

security_tooling.name: NIST AI Agent Standards Initiative
security_tooling.description: NIST CAISI initiative for AI agent standards and security. Covers agent identity, authorization, governance controls, and security research.
security_tooling.relevance: Emerging regulatory framework — complements FCA requirements with US-aligned standards for multinational organizations

security_tooling.name: Northflank: How to Sandbox AI Agents — Isolation Patterns 2026
security_tooling.description: Comprehensive sandbox isolation patterns: microVMs (Firecracker ~125ms boot, Kata), gVisor (syscall interception), standard containers. Network isolation, resource limits, zero-trust permissions. Sandboxed agents reduce security incidents 90%.
security_tooling.relevance: Security isolation patterns for regulated agent execution — extends Stripe devbox pattern with vendor-neutral options

fca_emi_compliance.name: FCA PS21/3: Building Operational Resilience
fca_emi_compliance.description: FCA policy on operational resilience — Important Business Services, impact tolerances, scenario testing
fca_emi_compliance.relevance: AI tools as critical dependencies in IBS; manual fallback pathways required

fca_emi_compliance.name: FCA/PRA SS1/23: Model Risk Management
fca_emi_compliance.description: Model risk management principles — AI-generated code as model output requiring inventory, validation, monitoring
fca_emi_compliance.relevance: Legal basis for tracking AI-generated code artifacts in model risk register

fca_emi_compliance.name: FCA SM&CR Regime
fca_emi_compliance.description: Senior Managers and Certification Regime — named accountability for regulated activities
fca_emi_compliance.relevance: Named Senior Manager must be accountable for AI tool governance; 'AI did it' is not a defense

fca_emi_compliance.name: FCA PS24/16: Critical Third Parties
fca_emi_compliance.description: Critical Third Party oversight regime — AI providers (Anthropic, OpenAI) may be designated CTPs
fca_emi_compliance.relevance: Portability requirement — dual-agent strategy (Claude Code + Codex) avoids vendor lock-in

fca_emi_compliance.name: ICO: Guidance on AI and Data Protection
fca_emi_compliance.description: UK Information Commissioner's Office guidance on AI systems and data protection
fca_emi_compliance.relevance: DPIA requirements, lawful basis for AI code generation, context files must not contain PII

fca_emi_compliance.name: FCA: Artificial Intelligence Update
fca_emi_compliance.description: FCA's latest position on AI use by regulated firms — expectations, good practice, supervisory focus
fca_emi_compliance.relevance: Regulatory direction on AI adoption in financial services; shapes governance requirements

fca_emi_compliance.name: EBA: Guidelines on AI Use in Financial Services
fca_emi_compliance.description: European Banking Authority guidelines on AI/ML models in financial services
fca_emi_compliance.relevance: EU counterpart to FCA guidance — relevant for firms operating across UK and EU

fca_emi_compliance.name: UK Government: Pro-Innovation Approach to AI Regulation
fca_emi_compliance.description: UK government white paper on AI regulation principles — context-specific, proportionate approach
fca_emi_compliance.relevance: Regulatory landscape for AI in UK — informs how FCA/PRA apply AI oversight

fca_emi_compliance.name: FINRA 2026: GenAI Regulatory Oversight — AI Agents Section
fca_emi_compliance.description: FINRA's first dedicated AI agents section. Defines agents as 'systems capable of autonomous actions on behalf of users.' Requires: prompt/output logging, model version tracking, human-in-the-loop review, agent action audit trails, guardrails to restrict behaviors.
fca_emi_compliance.relevance: US financial regulator guidance — validates FCA approach and adds specificity on audit trail requirements for AI coding agents

data_protection.name: IAPP: GDPR and Agentic AI
data_protection.description: IAPP analysis of GDPR implications for agentic AI systems — data processing, legal basis, safeguards
data_protection.relevance: Legal framework for AI agent data processing in development workflows

data_protection.name: PCI SSC: AI Principles for Payment Security
data_protection.description: PCI Security Standards Council — card data must never appear in agent context, prompts, or code
data_protection.relevance: Hard prohibition on card data in AI context — mandatory data-handling rule

data_protection.name: Anthropic: Data Processing Agreement (DPA)
data_protection.description: Anthropic's Data Processing Agreement for Claude and Claude Code usage
data_protection.relevance: Required contractual basis for using Claude Code under GDPR — verify DPA is in place

data_protection.name: OpenAI: Enterprise Data Processing
data_protection.description: OpenAI's enterprise privacy and data processing commitments for Codex
data_protection.relevance: Required for Codex usage under GDPR — verify terms meet FCA requirements

sdlc_and_devops.name: DORA: State of DevOps Report
sdlc_and_devops.description: Google DORA research on DevOps capabilities, metrics (deployment frequency, lead time, MTTR, change failure rate)
sdlc_and_devops.relevance: Baseline metrics for measuring SDLC compression — DORA metrics validate CDLC impact

sdlc_and_devops.name: GitHub Branch Protection Rules
sdlc_and_devops.description: GitHub branch protection configuration — required reviews, signed commits, status checks
sdlc_and_devops.relevance: Enforcement mechanism for separation of duties, signed commits, and compliance gates

sdlc_and_devops.name: Conventional Commits Specification
sdlc_and_devops.description: Standard for structured commit messages — type(scope): description
sdlc_and_devops.relevance: Commit convention referenced in compliance rules — enables automated changelogs and audit

sdlc_and_devops.name: GitHub CODEOWNERS
sdlc_and_devops.description: GitHub CODEOWNERS file for automatic review assignment based on file paths
sdlc_and_devops.relevance: Enforcement mechanism for requiring security/compliance review on sensitive directories

sdlc_and_devops.name: Git Signed Commits
sdlc_and_devops.description: Git documentation for GPG/SSH commit signing — cryptographic attribution
sdlc_and_devops.relevance: Mandatory audit trail requirement — all commits must be signed in regulated repos

sdlc_and_devops.name: GitHub Docs: Agentic Audit Log Events
sdlc_and_devops.description: Official GitHub documentation for agent-specific audit log events and enterprise observability of agent activity.
sdlc_and_devops.relevance: Primary-source reference for audit and accountability claims in regulated GitHub agent workflows

practitioner_insights.name: Addy Osmani: AI-Assisted Coding — Moving Beyond Autocomplete
practitioner_insights.description: Google Chrome engineer on structured approaches to AI coding — beyond simple suggestions
practitioner_insights.relevance: Practical patterns for moving from copilot-style to agent-style development

practitioner_insights.name: Kent Beck: AI-Assisted Software Development
practitioner_insights.description: Kent Beck's ongoing analysis of how AI changes software design — from TDD to AI-assisted patterns
practitioner_insights.relevance: Foundational thinking on how development practices evolve with AI agents

practitioner_insights.name: Lenny's Newsletter: How Top Engineering Teams Use AI Coding Agents
practitioner_insights.description: Case studies of engineering teams adopting AI coding agents at scale
practitioner_insights.relevance: Real-world adoption patterns and organizational change management insights

practitioner_insights.name: Cognition AI: Building with Devin — Lessons Learned
practitioner_insights.description: Cognition's blog on autonomous coding agents — context management, verification, limitations
practitioner_insights.relevance: Alternative agent perspective — validates need for structured context regardless of tool

practitioner_insights.name: Stripe: Minions — One-Shot End-to-End Coding Agents
practitioner_insights.description: Stripe's internal coding agents merging 1,000+ PRs/week across hundreds of millions of LOC. Covers pre-hydration context patterns, 400+ MCP Toolshed, devbox isolation, max-2-CI-rounds verification, and the 'good for humans = good for LLMs' principle.
practitioner_insights.relevance: Production-scale evidence for context engineering at a $1T+ payment company. Validates shift-left verification, agent rule files, selective CI, and human review separation of duties.

practitioner_insights.name: Block/Square: Goose — Open Source Coding Agent
practitioner_insights.description: Open-source coding agent by Block (Square). Stripe's Minions forked Goose as their core agent loop.
practitioner_insights.relevance: Alternative agent framework — demonstrates interleaved agent loops with deterministic code

practitioner_insights.name: Sourcegraph: Code Intelligence for AI Agents
practitioner_insights.description: Code intelligence platform used by Stripe Minions for codebase search and understanding via MCP
practitioner_insights.relevance: Context retrieval tool for large codebases — alternative to grep/ripgrep for 100M+ LOC repos

practitioner_insights.name: Cortex: The Engineering Leader's Guide to AI Tools for Developers in 2026
practitioner_insights.description: Engineering leader perspective on AI tool adoption. ROI measurement (connect to DORA metrics, not usage), governance (SOC 2, ISO 27001), internal developer portal integration, multi-tool strategy. Key insight: 'Well-defined engineering standards enable meaningful AI measurement.'
practitioner_insights.relevance: Leadership/management perspective on CE adoption — complements technical guidance with organizational strategy

practitioner_insights.name: Block/Square: Jack Dorsey AI-Driven 40% Workforce Reduction
practitioner_insights.description: Block cut 4,000 employees (40% of workforce) citing 'intelligence tools enabling smaller and flatter teams.' Gross profit up 24% YoY, stock rose 18%. Dorsey predicted most companies will follow within a year.
practitioner_insights.relevance: Highest-profile case study of AI-driven team restructuring — validates the parallel team hypothesis at scale

practitioner_insights.name: HBR: Companies Are Laying Off Workers Because of AI's Potential — Not Its Performance
practitioner_insights.description: Harvard Business Review analysis showing many AI-driven layoffs are based on anticipated capabilities, not proven performance. Critical counter-narrative to the restructuring hype.
practitioner_insights.relevance: Essential caveat for team transformation — challenges the assumption that AI has already proven itself enough to justify team cuts

practitioner_insights.name: SF Standard: AI Writes the Code Now — What's Left for Software Engineers?
practitioner_insights.description: Graduate hiring at top 15 US tech companies down 55% since 2019. UC CS enrollment declining for first time since dot-com era. One engineer generates 100K+ lines across 6 projects in 2 weeks using Claude Code.
practitioner_insights.relevance: Labor market evidence for the shift — data points on engineering role transformation and hiring trends

practitioner_insights.name: Harvard/P&G: When AI Joins the Team, Better Ideas Surface
practitioner_insights.description: Field experiment at P&G: teams with AI produced 3x more top-10% quality ideas. Individuals with AI matched quality of 2-person human teams without AI. AI expanded problem-solving expertise across broader employee populations.
practitioner_insights.relevance: Rigorous experimental evidence that AI amplifies team capability — supports the 'smaller team + AI' hypothesis

practitioner_insights.name: OpenAI: Building an AI-Native Engineering Team
practitioner_insights.description: OpenAI's guide to structuring engineering teams around Codex and AI coding agents. Covers role definitions, workflow patterns, and team sizing for AI-native organizations.
practitioner_insights.relevance: Authoritative guidance on AI-native team structure from the Codex platform provider

practitioner_insights.name: VC Corner: The Billion-Dollar Startup Formula — AI-Driven Small Teams Beating Giants
practitioner_insights.description: Midjourney ($200M ARR, 10 people), Cursor ($100M ARR, 20 people), BuiltWith ($14M/year, 1 person). Solo founders = 35% of 2024 startups. 1 AI engineer ≈ 5 traditional engineers.
practitioner_insights.relevance: Venture capital perspective on AI-native team economics — concrete revenue/headcount ratios
